# rem — CLI for macOS Reminders

rem is an open-source command-line interface for macOS Reminders, written in Go. It uses go-eventkit (github.com/BRO3886/go-eventkit) to bridge Apple's EventKit framework directly into the Go binary via cgo and Objective-C, achieving sub-200ms reads and writes for all operations. This is 462x faster than the AppleScript/JXA approach (which takes 60+ seconds for large lists due to Apple Event IPC overhead).

- GitHub: https://github.com/BRO3886/rem
- Website: https://rem.sidv.dev/
- Install: curl -fsSL https://rem.sidv.dev/install | bash
- Go module: github.com/BRO3886/rem
- License: MIT
- Author: Siddharth Verma (https://sidv.dev)
- macOS only (requires macOS 10.12+, EventKit framework)


## Installation

### Recommended (install script)
curl -fsSL https://rem.sidv.dev/install | bash

### Go install (requires Go + Xcode CLT)
go install github.com/BRO3886/rem/cmd/rem@latest

### Manual (from GitHub Releases)
curl -LO https://github.com/BRO3886/rem/releases/latest/download/rem-darwin-arm64.tar.gz
tar xzf rem-darwin-arm64.tar.gz
sudo mv rem /usr/local/bin/rem
# Use rem-darwin-amd64.tar.gz for Intel Macs


## Commands (all 19)

rem add "title" [--due DATE] [--priority high|medium|low|none] [--list NAME] [--notes TEXT] [--url URL] [--flagged] [-i]
rem list [--list NAME] [--incomplete] [--completed] [--flagged] [--due-before DATE] [--due-after DATE] [-s QUERY]
rem show ID
rem update ID [--name TEXT] [--due DATE] [--priority LEVEL] [--notes TEXT] [--url URL] [--flagged true|false] [-i]
rem complete ID
rem uncomplete ID
rem flag ID
rem unflag ID
rem delete ID [--force]
rem search QUERY [--list NAME] [--incomplete]
rem stats
rem overdue
rem upcoming [--days N]
rem lists [--count]
rem list-mgmt create NAME
rem list-mgmt rename OLD NEW
rem list-mgmt delete NAME [--force]
rem export [--list NAME] [--format json|csv] [--output-file PATH] [--incomplete]
rem import FILE [--list NAME] [--dry-run]
rem interactive  (alias: rem i)
rem skills install [--agent claude|codex|openclaw|all]
rem skills uninstall [--agent claude|codex|openclaw|all]
rem skills status
rem version

Global flags: -o table|json|plain, --no-color, -h


## Natural Language Date Formats

rem understands these date patterns (used with --due and --remind):
- Relative: "in 2 days", "in 3 hours", "in 30 minutes"
- Named: "today", "tomorrow", "yesterday"
- Day of week: "next monday", "next friday"
- Special: "eod" (5pm today), "eow" (Friday 5pm), "next week", "next month"
- Compound: "next friday at 2pm", "tomorrow at 3:30pm"
- Time only: "5pm", "17:00", "3:30pm"
- ISO 8601: "2026-02-15", "2026-02-15T14:30:00"
- US format: "02/15/2026", "2/15"
- Named month: "Feb 15", "February 15, 2026"


## Architecture

rem uses go-eventkit (github.com/BRO3886/go-eventkit v0.2.1) for ALL reads and writes:
- Reminder CRUD: create, read, update, delete, complete/uncomplete via EventKit
- List CRUD: create, rename, delete via EventKit
- Performance: <200ms for all EventKit operations
- AppleScript fallback only for: flag/unflag operations, default list name query (EventKit doesn't expose isFlagged)

Stack:
- cmd/rem/commands/ — Cobra CLI commands
- internal/service/ — Service layer wrapping go-eventkit
- internal/reminder/ — Domain models (Reminder, List, Priority)
- internal/parser/ — Custom natural language date parser (no external deps)
- internal/export/ — JSON/CSV import/export
- internal/skills/ — Agent skill install/uninstall/status
- internal/update/ — Background update check (GitHub releases, 24h cache)


## Performance Benchmarks (224 reminders, 12 lists)

rem list (all 224): 0.13s  (vs ~60s with JXA — 462x faster)
rem list --incomplete: 0.11s
rem search "query": 0.11s  (vs ~60s with JXA — 545x faster)
rem stats: 0.17s  (vs ~68s with JXA — 400x faster)
rem lists: 0.12s  (vs 8.3s with JXA — 69x faster)
rem show (prefix): 0.11s
rem export --format json: 0.12s

EventKit is an in-process framework (direct SQLite access, no IPC).
AppleScript/JXA sends Apple Events over Mach IPC — one event per property per reminder.


## AI Agent Skills

rem ships with an embedded agent skill that teaches AI coding assistants how to use it:

rem skills install                  # Interactive picker (Claude, Codex, OpenClaw)
rem skills install --agent claude   # Claude Code only (~/.claude/skills/rem-cli/)
rem skills install --agent codex    # Codex CLI only (~/.agents/skills/rem-cli/)
rem skills install --agent openclaw # OpenClaw only (~/.openclaw/skills/rem-cli/)
rem skills install --agent all      # All supported agents
rem skills status                   # Show installation status
rem skills uninstall --agent all    # Remove skills


## Output Formats

Every command supports three output formats:
-o table   (default) — colored ASCII table
-o json    — machine-readable JSON, suitable for piping to jq
-o plain   — one result per line, for shell scripting

Environment: NO_COLOR=1 disables colors. REM_NO_UPDATE_CHECK=1 disables background update checks.


## Examples

# Add a reminder with natural language date
rem add "Ship v2.0" --due friday --priority high --list Work

# List all incomplete reminders
rem list --incomplete

# Complete a reminder by short ID
rem complete 6ECE

# Search across all lists
rem search "quarterly review" --incomplete

# Export all Work reminders to JSON
rem export --list Work --format json --output-file work.json

# Import from CSV
rem import tasks.csv --list Imported --dry-run

# Interactive add
rem add -i

# View stats
rem stats

# Manage lists
rem lm create "Projects"
rem lm rename "Projects" "Active Projects"
rem lists --count
